refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the system is capable of performing its intended mission to total operational time, accounting for planned maintenance, unplanned downtime, and safety-driven shutdowns.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts customer satisfaction and mission success. Refining it explicitly defines the measurement basis (availability ratio), the target threshold (≥0.97), the evaluation period scope, and acknowledges that availability is a system-level emergent property dependent on reliability, maintainability, and safety-driven operational constraints. High safety criticality requires that safety-driven shutdowns are counted as legitimate downtime, not failures.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Collect operational telemetry (uptime/downtime events, root causes, duration) across the evaluation period; calculate availability as (total_time - downtime) / total_time; compare against ≥0.97 threshold; stratify by failure mode (reliability, maintenance, safety) to identify improvement priorities.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve a mean time between failures (MTBF) target that, combined with maintenance and safety shutdown rates, supports the system-level availability target.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network link availability and command/telemetry latency within bounds that do not degrade overall system availability.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall execute safety-driven shutdowns and recovery procedures in a manner that minimizes unplanned downtime while maintaining safety integrity.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide diagnostic and predictive maintenance capabilities to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR) and unplanned downtime.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundancy and fault tolerance in critical subsystems (Vehicle propulsion, Teleoperation network, Safety Services logic) to reduce unplanned downtime; couple with predictive maintenance and health monitoring to reduce MTTR.

DP-B: Establish a tiered availability architecture: high-reliability core (Vehicle drive/control), resilient teleoperation (dual network paths, failover), and safety-critical shutdown logic with automatic recovery; monitor and log all downtime events for continuous improvement.

DP-C: Design the system with modular, replaceable components (Vehicle modules, Teleoperation hardware, Safety Services firmware) to enable rapid repair and minimize MTTR; implement condition-based maintenance triggers via telemetry.

DP-D: Allocate availability budgets to each subsystem (Vehicle MTBF target, Teleoperation uptime target, Safety Services availability target) and enforce design reviews and testing to meet allocated targets.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Establish a tiered availability architecture with high-reliability core subsystems (Vehicle drive/control), resilient teleoperation (dual network paths, failover), and safety-critical shutdown logic with automatic recovery; implement comprehensive telemetry-driven monitoring and event logging to enable root-cause analysis and continuous improvement of availability.

potential_sources: Teleoperation network resilience (dual paths) is decoupled from Vehicle reliability via failover logic; Safety Services shutdown logic is decoupled from normal operation via independent safety channels.
Monitoring and logging (Telemetry interface) is a cross-cutting concern but does not create functional coupling if implemented as a passive observer.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: reliability (Vehicle), resilience (Teleoperation), safety (Safety Services), and observability (Telemetry). The tiered architecture allows independent optimization of each subsystem.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Dual network paths for Teleoperation are feasible and cost-effective within project constraints.
Assumption: Safety Services can execute independent shutdown logic without blocking normal operation recovery.
Risk: Automatic recovery logic may mask underlying failures if not properly validated; requires rigorous testing and safety case review.
Risk: Telemetry overhead (logging, transmission) may impact real-time performance if not carefully designed; requires bandwidth and latency analysis.
Risk: Availability target of ≥0.97 may be unachievable with current technology or cost constraints; requires feasibility study and trade-off analysis.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) redundancy strategy for each subsystem (e.g., 1oo2 for Teleoperation, N-version for Safety Services), (2) failover/recovery time budgets (e.g., <5 seconds for Teleoperation failover), (3) telemetry data retention and analysis procedures, and (4) acceptance criteria for availability testing (e.g., 95% confidence interval, minimum sample size).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates availability across all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). It owns the availability KPI measurement, monitoring infrastructure, and cross-subsystem coordination logic (e.g., failover triggers, recovery sequencing).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits availability metrics, downtime events, root-cause data, and health status to external monitoring/analytics systems for KPI tracking and continuous improvement.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives mission tasking and operational mode commands; availability architecture must support graceful degradation and recovery during mode transitions.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates with Safety Services to receive safety alerts and execute coordinated shutdown/recovery; also emits availability-related safety status (e.g., degraded mode warnings).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level availability KPI, monitoring, and cross-subsystem coordination. Availability is an emergent property of the integrated system, not a single subsystem concern. High containment because the FR is explicitly about the overall solution. Low diffusion because monitoring and coordination logic can be cleanly encapsulated at the system level.

Vehicle owns reliability (MTBF) and maintainability (MTTR) of propulsion/control, which are necessary but not sufficient for system availability. Availability also depends on Teleoperation resilience and Safety Services logic. Assigning the FR to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility and obscure cross-subsystem dependencies.

Teleoperation owns network resilience and failover, which are critical for availability but only one component. Availability also depends on Vehicle reliability and Safety Services. Assigning the FR to Teleoperation alone would create false ownership and miss Vehicle/Safety dependencies.

Safety Services owns safety-critical shutdown and recovery logic, which impacts availability but is not the primary driver. Availability is primarily driven by reliability and resilience, not safety. Assigning the FR to Safety Services alone would misalign ownership and create diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct owner of CR-NGHE-004 because: (1) Availability is a system-level emergent property that depends on the integration and coordination of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) The measurement, monitoring, and KPI tracking are system-level concerns; (3) Cross-subsystem failover and recovery logic must be coordinated at the system level; (4) Assigning to any single subsystem would create diffusion and obscure dependencies.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if it accumulates too much monitoring and coordination logic; requires careful interface design and delegation to subsystems for subsystem-specific health checks.

Risk: Availability measurement requires data from all subsystems (Vehicle uptime, Teleoperation link status, Safety Services shutdown events); requires well-defined telemetry contracts and data quality assurance.
Risk: Cross-subsystem failover logic may create tight coupling if not carefully abstracted; requires clear failover state machines and event-driven architecture.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution module boundary to explicitly define: (1) Availability monitoring and KPI calculation logic (owned by NGHE Solution), (2) Subsystem health check interfaces (each subsystem reports health status via Telemetry), (3) Failover and recovery state machines (owned by NGHE Solution, triggered by subsystem health events), and (4) Telemetry data contracts (schema, frequency, retention) to ensure data quality and traceability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (tiered availability architecture with redundancy, resilience, and monitoring) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). The DP specifies how to achieve the target through redundancy (Vehicle reliability), resilience (Teleoperation failover), safety-driven shutdown logic, and continuous monitoring. The DP is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides sufficient guidance for detailed design without prescribing implementation details.

diffusion_risks: Risk: Telemetry interface may become a bottleneck if monitoring logic is not carefully designed; requires bandwidth and latency analysis.
Risk: Failover and recovery logic may be distributed across NGHE Solution and subsystems if not clearly owned; requires explicit state machine documentation and interface contracts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner of system-level availability KPI and cross-subsystem coordination. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own subsystem-specific reliability, resilience, and safety logic, which they report via Telemetry. This separation of concerns minimizes diffusion and enables independent optimization.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability can be verified by collecting operational telemetry (uptime/downtime events) over the evaluation period and calculating the availability ratio. Requires: (1) well-defined telemetry contracts, (2) reliable event logging and timestamping, (3) root-cause classification procedures, and (4) statistical analysis to confirm ≥0.97 with acceptable confidence interval.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The FR is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level KPI owner. Sub-FRs are allocated to subsystems (Vehicle MTBF, Teleoperation uptime, Safety Services availability). Allocation is clear and traceable; each subsystem has explicit targets that, when met, support the system-level availability target.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to specify redundancy strategies, failover time budgets, and telemetry data contracts.
Refine NGHE Solution module boundary to explicitly define monitoring, KPI calculation, and failover logic ownership.
Develop availability allocation spreadsheet mapping system-level target (≥0.97) to subsystem targets (Vehicle MTBF, Teleoperation uptime, Safety Services availability).
Establish telemetry data quality assurance procedures to ensure accurate availability measurement.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that is not atomic and requires decomposition into subsystem-level functional requirements. The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass the FR-DP-IM gates. However, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through FR-NGHE-004.4) require detailed design and allocation to subsystems. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify subsystem-specific availability targets, design parameters, and implementation modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The Vehicle subsystem shall achieve a mean time between failures (MTBF) target of [X hours] for propulsion and control systems, supporting the system-level availability target of ≥0.97.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain network link availability of ≥0.99 and command/telemetry latency of <[Y] ms, supporting the system-level availability target.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall execute safety-driven shutdowns and automatic recovery within [Z] seconds, minimizing unplanned downtime while maintaining safety integrity.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide predictive maintenance and health monitoring capabilities to reduce mean time to repair (MTTR) by [P]% and support continuous availability improvement.

assumptions: Availability is measured as (total_time - downtime) / total_time over the customer-defined evaluation period.
Safety-driven shutdowns are counted as legitimate downtime, not failures.
Dual network paths for Teleoperation are feasible and cost-effective.
Automatic recovery logic can be validated and does not mask underlying failures.
Telemetry overhead does not significantly impact real-time performance.
Availability target of ≥0.97 is achievable with current technology and cost constraints (to be validated in feasibility study).
open_risks: Automatic recovery logic may mask underlying failures if not properly validated; requires rigorous testing and safety case review.
Telemetry overhead may impact real-time performance; requires bandwidth and latency analysis.
Availability target may be unachievable with current technology or cost constraints; requires feasibility study.
NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if monitoring and coordination logic is not carefully designed; requires interface design review.
Cross-subsystem failover logic may create tight coupling; requires state machine documentation and interface contracts.
evidence_needed: Feasibility study confirming ≥0.97 availability is achievable with proposed architecture.
Availability allocation spreadsheet mapping system-level target to subsystem targets.
Telemetry data quality assurance procedures and validation results.
Failover and recovery state machines with time budgets and acceptance criteria.
Safety case review confirming automatic recovery logic does not compromise safety.
Operational test results demonstrating ≥0.97 availability over evaluation period.
